name: Hippotrix
short_description: Creative studio making exploratory, play-led apps for children
about:
Hippotrix works alongside the broader D Fi Foe umbrella of creatives, whose partners had already spent roughly 15 years commissioning cross-disciplinary work before Hippotrix’s app products channelled those lessons directly into handheld software. That earlier track record included education-heavy projects with venues such as London’s Southbank Centre, English National Opera, and Scottish Ballet, plus youth filmmaking around the Olympic “Welcoming the world” programme for London 2012.

Hippotrix Ltd turns that grounding into exploratory play apps—“digital toys” for younger audiences—with research-led interaction design rooted in facilitation rather than bombardment with rewards. Since the studio formally launched in 2013 many releases have paired with artists and educators to foreground gentle pacing, tactile interfaces, and space for improvisation.

The team stitches together illustration, animation, sound design, and code in-house, favouring crafty presentation over churn-heavy live-service pacing.
